danger_signals.visual_form: Geometric/sphere-based robot constructs with hostile design language
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats under WarHead's control; player must avoid or destroy

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards integrated into level design
danger_signals.meaning: Collision damage; areas to avoid or navigate carefully
Proximity to death state; urgent need to find health pickups
Failure condition; player must reach goal before expiration

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible orb pickups scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Restore player health; safe to approach and collect

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple branching routes within large level spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and choice; freedom to explore safer alternative routes

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible transformation items and equipment drops
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced capabilities (helicopter form, drills, bombs); temporary safety advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Geometric vector-based character composed of 23 synchronized sprite pieces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Clean, technological aesthetic aligned with orbot design language

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Raster/WarHead: higher-level supervisory robot accidentally fused with nuclear warhead control systems
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot enemies throughout levels reflect WarHead's command; geometric orbot designs corrupted by his influence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hostile robotic installations; weaponized orbot infrastructure; polluted Earth aesthetic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied through narrative; currently in outerspace migration due to Earth pollution
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pollution-damaged Earth environment; cleanup mission infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level environments reflect post-apocalyptic pollution damage; degraded landscapes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Humanity forced to migrate to outerspace; orbots deployed as cleanup agents

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hostile robot enemies throughout levels; weaponized orbot structures; altered environmental aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All robots except Vectorman now under WarHead command; aggressive enemy AI; level-to-level escalation of threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player character distinct from corrupted enemy robots through design and behavior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vectorman operates independently; must actively oppose all other robots; sole agent of resistance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Countdown timer visible in HUD; timed level structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced player progression; no pausable exploration; urgency throughout gameplay

environmental_storytelling.example: Polluted Earth landscape as visual backdrop and hazard source; environment itself reflects humanity's destructive impact
Vector-based geometric design language for all robots and machinery; sphere/orb construction vocabulary communicates 'synthetic life'
16 levels structured as escalating confrontation with WarHead's forces; geographic progression through corrupted infrastructure
Human presence absent from playable levels; all NPCs are robots; communicates depopulation and isolation narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Capable of 8-directional laser projectile attack; traversal through running/jumping; responsive to player input
Helicopter form enables flight mobility; vehicular form grants speed boost; alternative weapon systems (drills, bombs) change interaction with environment and enemies
Increased vulnerability; visual life bar indicates critical condition; single additional damage results in life loss and level restart
Hostile patrol/attack patterns; pursue player; deal damage on contact; remain until destroyed
Deal damage on collision; restrict player movement; function as obstacle/puzzle elements within level design

notes: Vectorman communicates its political sci-fi premise (ecological collapse, robot uprising, authoritarian control by WarHead) primarily through narrative exposition and environmental design rather than explicit visual faction indicators. The vector-based geometric aesthetic creates visual distinction between the synthetic world (robots, machinery) and the implied absent human world. World-state is conveyed through: (1) backdrop pollution imagery, (2) escalating robot enemy presence, (3) time-pressure mechanics reinforcing urgency, (4) transformative powerups enabling adaptation to environmental challenges. Danger is primarily communicated through enemy presence and health meter depletion; safety through collectible restoration items. The 'Vector Piece Animation' technique (23-sprite character composition) creates a distinctly recognizable protagonist silhouette that contrasts with generic enemy robot designs, supporting narrative of Vectorman's unique/isolated status.
